_comment: Team-wide Claude Code settings for Project Nova. COMMITTED. Personal overrides go in settings.local.json (gitignored). Read CLAUDE.md before adjusting this file.

env._comment: Default subagent model = Haiku 4.5. Detailed plans + Haiku implementer = competent + cheap (Superpowers 4 / Jesse Vincent). Per-dispatch override `model: "sonnet"` for cognitive-layer subagent tasks. Falls back to Sonnet automatically if Haiku reports out-of-depth.

_hooks_explanation: Pre-commit framework (.pre-commit-config.yaml) handles formatting + linting + gitleaks on commit; CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) handles full test/lint/type runs on push. The pre-push command hook is a fast deterministic gate: regex secret grep + diff stat + PR-cadence warning + post-merge /clear hint. No LLM agent runs at push time — token cost ~$0/push. Quality judgment (logic bugs, missing tests, design issues) is handled in-session by /commit-push-pr which auto-dispatches Opus code-reviewer + security-reviewer subagents (Max quota, not API) before every non-doc commit. CI LLM review (claude-review.yml) has been removed — replaced by in-session Opus review. PostToolUse formatters intentionally NOT added — they create read/write race conditions with Claude's edit cycle. Personal hooks can be added in settings.local.json.

hooks.command: up=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name @{u} 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$up" ] && exit 0; n=$(git rev-list @{u}..HEAD --count 2>/dev/null || echo 0); [ "${n:-0}" = "0" ] && exit 0; sec=$(git diff @{u}..HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^[+].*(ANTHROPIC_API_KEY|OPENAI_API_KEY|sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}|AIza[A-Za-z0-9_-]{35})' | head -3); [ -n "$sec" ] && printf '{"continue":false,"stopReason":"possible API key in diff","systemMessage":"API key pattern found — inspect before pushing"}' && exit 0; risky=$(git diff @{u}..HEAD --name-only 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'nova_agent/(llm|perception|memory)|AgentEvent|bus' | tr '\n' ' '); stat=$(git diff @{u}..HEAD --stat 2>/dev/null | tail -1); [ -n "$risky" ] && printf '{"continue":true,"systemMessage":"%s commits (%s). Cognitive paths touched: %s"}' "$n" "$stat" "$risky" || printf '{"continue":true,"systemMessage":"%s commits (%s)"}' "$n" "$stat"

{ ahead=$(git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "${ahead:-0}" -gt 30 ]; then branch=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$branch" ] && [ "$branch" != "main" ]; then pr_open=$(gh pr list --head "$branch" --state open --json number 2>/dev/null | grep -c number || echo 0); if [ "${pr_open:-0}" = "0" ]; then printf '{"systemMessage":"PR overdue: %s commits ahead of origin/main on %s without an open PR. Open one before the merge cost spirals — see ADR-0007-style cadence. (gh pr create --base main)"}' "$ahead" "$branch"; fi; fi; fi; } 2>/dev/null

{ ahead=$(git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo 0); branch=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ "${ahead:-0}" = "0" ] && [ -n "$branch" ] && [ "$branch" != "main" ]; then recent_merge=$(gh pr list --state merged --search "head:$branch" --limit 1 --json mergedAt --jq '.[0].mergedAt // ""' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$recent_merge" ]; then cutoff=$(date -u -v-30M +%FT%TZ 2>/dev/null || date -u -d "30 minutes ago" +%FT%TZ 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$cutoff" ] && [ "$recent_merge" \> "$cutoff" ]; then printf '{"systemMessage":"PR just merged on %s, branch synced with origin/main. Natural break point — consider /clear with curated handoff before starting the next unit. (Template in workflow.md Session hygiene section.)"}' "$branch"; fi; fi; fi; } 2>/dev/null

branch=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$branch" ] && exit 0; prs=$(gh pr list --head "$branch" --state open --json number,title 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$prs" ] && [ "$(echo "$prs" | jq 'length')" -gt 0 ] && jq -n --argjson prs "$prs" '{systemMessage: ("WARNING: Open PR(s) on current branch (\($prs | length)):
" + ($prs | map("  - PR #\(.number) — \(.title)") | join("
")) + "

Before committing more work: confirm the new unit belongs in the existing PR, or wait for merge first. Commits-stacked-on-open-PR is the workflow.md "one-big-PR drift trap."")}'
hooks.statusMessage: Checking open PRs on current branch...
